# OPC UA Laravel Client

> Laravel integration for OPC UA built on opcua-client and opcua-session-manager. Provides a Facade, .env-based configuration, named connections, and an Artisan command for the session manager daemon.

## Use Cases

- Read and write process variables from PLCs, SCADA systems, sensors via a Laravel Facade
- Browse OPC UA address spaces and resolve paths from Laravel controllers, jobs, or commands
- Subscribe to data changes and events with session persistence across HTTP requests
- Call methods on OPC UA servers from a Laravel application
- Query historical data (raw, processed, at-time) from historians

## Key Features

- Facade (`Opcua::read()`, `Opcua::browse()`, etc.) with full IDE autocompletion
- Named connections — define multiple OPC UA servers in config/opcua.php
- Ad-hoc connections — Opcua::connectTo() for runtime endpoints
- Transparent session management — daemon available → ManagedClient; not → direct Client
- String NodeIds everywhere — 'i=2259', 'ns=2;s=MyNode'
- Fluent Builder API — readMulti(), writeMulti(), createMonitoredItems(), translateBrowsePaths()
- PSR-3 Logging — Laravel log channel injected automatically
- PSR-14 Events — 47 dispatched events covering every OPC UA operation for observability
- PSR-16 Caching — Laravel cache store injected automatically, per-call useCache
- Write auto-detection — omit the type parameter in write() and let the client detect the correct OPC UA type
- Read metadata cache — cached node metadata avoids redundant reads; refresh with read($nodeId, refresh: true)
- Trust store — FileTrustStore with configurable TrustPolicy, auto-accept, certificate management
- Type Discovery — discoverDataTypes() for server-defined structured types
- Subscription management — createMonitoredItems(), modifyMonitoredItems(), setTriggering(), transferSubscriptions()
- MockClient for testing — register handlers, assert calls, no server needed
- Timeout, auto-retry, automatic batching via config or fluent API
- Recursive browsing with depth control and cycle detection
- Path resolution — resolveNodeId('/Objects/Server/ServerStatus')
- 6 security policies, 3 auth modes, auto-generated certificates, certificate trust management
- Historical data — raw, processed, and at-time queries
- All service responses return typed DTOs (SubscriptionResult, CallResult, BrowseResultSet, MonitoredItemModifyResult, SetTriggeringResult, ExtensionObject, etc.)

## Installation

```bash
composer require php-opcua/laravel-opcua
```

## Quick Example

```php
use PhpOpcua\LaravelOpcua\Facades\Opcua;

$client = Opcua::connect();
$value = $client->read('i=2259');
echo $value->getValue(); // 0 = Running
$client->disconnect();
```

## Fluent Builder API

```php
$results = $client->readMulti()
    ->node('i=2259')->value()
    ->node('ns=2;i=1001')->displayName()
    ->execute();
```

## Session Manager

```bash
php artisan opcua:session --log-channel=stack --cache-store=redis
```

## Main Classes

- PhpOpcua\LaravelOpcua\OpcuaManager — connection management, client creation, configuration
- PhpOpcua\LaravelOpcua\Facades\Opcua — static Facade with full PHPDoc
- PhpOpcua\LaravelOpcua\OpcuaServiceProvider — service container registration
- PhpOpcua\LaravelOpcua\Commands\SessionCommand — Artisan command for daemon

## Configuration Keys

Standard: endpoint, security_policy, security_mode, username, password, client_certificate, client_key, ca_certificate, user_certificate, user_key, timeout, auto_retry, batch_size, browse_max_depth
New in v4: trust_store_path, trust_policy, auto_accept, auto_accept_force, auto_detect_write_type, read_metadata_cache

## Key Method Signatures

- read(string|NodeId $nodeId, bool $refresh = false): DataValue
- write(string|NodeId $nodeId, mixed $value, ?BuiltinType $type = null): int
- modifyMonitoredItems(int $subscriptionId, array $itemsToModify): MonitoredItemModifyResult[]
- setTriggering(int $subscriptionId, int $triggeringItemId, array $linksToAdd = [], array $linksToRemove = []): SetTriggeringResult
- trustCertificate(string $certificate): void
- untrustCertificate(string $certificate): void

## Trust Store

FileTrustStore manages trusted/rejected server certificates on disk. Configured via trust_store_path, trust_policy (TrustPolicy enum), auto_accept, and auto_accept_force. Methods: trustCertificate(), untrustCertificate(). Throws UntrustedCertificateException when a server certificate is not trusted.

## PSR-14 Events

47 events dispatched via Laravel's event dispatcher. Events cover: connection lifecycle, read/write, browse, subscriptions, method calls, history, discovery, trust store operations, and errors.

## New Exceptions

- UntrustedCertificateException — server certificate not in the trust store
- WriteTypeDetectionException — auto-detection of write type failed
- WriteTypeMismatchException — detected type does not match the value

## Related Packages

- php-opcua/opcua-client — Pure PHP OPC UA client (core protocol)
- php-opcua/opcua-session-manager — Daemon-based session persistence
- php-opcua/opcua-cli — CLI tool for OPC UA operations
- php-opcua/opcua-client-nodeset — Pre-generated PHP types from OPC Foundation companion specifications
- php-opcua/uanetstandard-test-suite — Docker-based OPC UA test servers (UA-.NETStandard)

## Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2
- ext-openssl
- Laravel 11.x or 12.x
